Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (all through Court Call):
Peter Chao Esq. 510-832-8899 of Caho & Lopez for Plaintiff Jay Wang.
Baldwin Lee Esq. 415-837-1515 of Allen Matkins Leck Gamble Mallory & Natsis LLP for Defendant Oracle America.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues the details on Judge Wong's order. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ORACLE AMERICA, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendant Oracle America, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted and the action is stayed. The January 25, 2021 trial date is vacated. Defendant shows that in 2009, the parties entered into a written arbitration agreement that mandates arbitration of the employment claims alleged in the complaint. Plaintiff's contention that the agreement is unconscionable is unsupported by any admissible evidence, citation to authority, or intelligible argument. Plaintiff's contention that the petition is untimely lacks merit. This action was filed on December 24, 2019, and defendant filed an answer on February 24, 2020.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

A proposed order is submitted by email. Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR# 6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
